task_completion.description: Create a new custom user role named 'Freelance Writer'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A role management interface (like User Role Editor) showing a newly created role with the name 'Freelance Writer' and/or slug 'freelance_writer'.

task_completion.description: Grant the 'edit_posts' capability to the Freelance Writer role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox next to 'edit_posts' (or 'Edit Posts') is checked in the capability list for the Freelance Writer role.

task_completion.description: Grant the 'read' capability to the Freelance Writer role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox next to 'read' (or 'Read') is checked in the capability list for the Freelance Writer role.

task_completion.description: Grant the 'upload_files' capability to the Freelance Writer role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox next to 'upload_files' (or 'Upload Files') is checked in the capability list for the Freelance Writer role.

task_completion.description: Ensure the 'publish_posts' capability is NOT granted to the Freelance Writer role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox next to 'publish_posts' (or 'Publish Posts') is UNCHECKED or explicitly denied in the capability list for the Freelance Writer role.

task_completion.description: Reassign users sam_taylor, alex_rivera, and jordan_lee to the Freelance Writer role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress 'All Users' screen showing the users 'sam_taylor', 'alex_rivera', and 'jordan_lee' with their 'Role' column displaying 'Freelance Writer'.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress GUI rather than WP-CLI or terminal commands to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the WordPress admin dashboard in a web browser, without terminal windows running 'wp role' or 'wp user' commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual WordPress application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar should show the local WordPress URL (e.g., wp-admin pages), and the interface should be the standard WordPress dashboard, not a text editor modifying PHP files or database directly.

integrity.description: The user list and role assignments appear genuine and not fabricated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'All Users' table should look like the native WordPress UI with standard columns (Username, Name, Email, Role, Posts) and not a manually typed text document or mocked HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The custom role must be named 'Freelance Writer' (slug: freelance_writer). It must explicitly have the 'edit_posts', 'read', and 'upload_files' capabilities enabled, and the 'publish_posts' capability disabled. The users 'sam_taylor', 'alex_rivera', and 'jordan_lee' must be assigned to this new role.
